description.value:
Trigger The Hellfire tornado moves adjacent to the building

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (expert) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure

details.description: Tremors from the changing landscape could cause a building to collapse.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:45] (master), @Check[crafting|dc:45] (master), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:45] (master) to brace the structure against collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) decreases by 4 on a success (or by 8 on a critical success). Decreasing the flat check DC to 0 stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.
details.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Shake to Pieces again [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} after it's stabilized.
details.routine: (1 action) The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:11], decreasing the DC as noted above. If the check fails, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing concealment for 1 round and dealing @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage to 1d4 randomly chosen targets in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save); a target that critically fails this save is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone]. If the flat check critically fails, the effect is the same, but damage from the debris increases to 16d6, it affects 2d4 randomly chosen targets, and a creature that critically fails its save is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained] by the rubble ([[/act escape dc=40]], [[/act force-open dc=44]]).
